other_observers: observed with Jim Beatty; I believe the bird was first found by Nick Komar
description: the bird was a large loon, the same size or larger than a Common Loon. The overall shape was quite bulky (for a loon), with a lumpy head with a very large lump above the eye, and a flat crown behind the eye contributing to this look. The neck was quite thick. The bill was very small for a YBLO, with the smallest gonydeal angle I've ever seen on this species. It was completely a straw yellow color, incluing the culmen. The eye was reddish, and looked quite small on the large head. The bird held it's bill above the horizontal for most of the observation.  The crown, nape, and sides of the neck were a straw-brownish color. The face, chin, and throat were white. Becuase of the white face and pale brown surrounding the face, the bird had a fairly "blank" look when compared to the darker head of COLO. The back was a medium to dark brown, with bold white feather edges giving the appearance of bold white bars down the length of the back.Behaviors: The bird was seen loafing around on the reservoir, and only actively diving towards the end of the observation.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenal
photographer: Photographer: Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer
